fields.name: gt
fields.description: Return only events whose `effective_at` (Unix seconds) is greater than this value.

fields.name: gte
fields.description: Return only events whose `effective_at` (Unix seconds) is greater than or equal to this value.

fields.name: lt
fields.description: Return only events whose `effective_at` (Unix seconds) is less than this value.

fields.name: lte
fields.description: Return only events whose `effective_at` (Unix seconds) is less than or equal to this value.

methods.description: List user actions and configuration changes within this organization.

queryParams.name: effective_at
queryParams.description: Return only events whose `effective_at` (Unix seconds) is in this range.

queryParams.name: project_ids
queryParams.description: Return only events for these projects.

queryParams.name: event_types
queryParams.description: Return only events with a `type` in one of these values. For example, `project.created`. For all options, see the documentation for the [audit log object](/docs/api-reference/audit-logs/object).

queryParams.name: actor_ids
queryParams.description: Return only events performed by these actors. Can be a user ID, a service account ID, or an api key tracking ID.

queryParams.name: actor_emails
queryParams.description: Return only events performed by users with these emails.

queryParams.name: resource_ids
queryParams.description: Return only events performed on these targets. For example, a project ID updated.

queryParams.name: limit
queryParams.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 20.

queryParams.name: after
queryParams.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with obj_foo, your subsequent call can include after=obj_foo in order to fetch the next page of the list.

queryParams.name: before
queryParams.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with obj_foo, your subsequent call can include before=obj_foo in order to fetch the previous page of the list.
